entry.title: Hooded Girl that Hides
entry.content:
PaperFaces portrait of Danya W. inspired by a photograph on Sktchy.

Lately I’ve been drawn to images of figures partially obscured by shadow. I find it easier to work them into a composition that covers the entirety of Paper’s digital canvas.

The original illustration is available on Mix if you want to rework it or get a closer look.

Stylus: Pencil by FiftyThree
App: Paper by FiftyThree

Hooded Girl that Hides was originally published by at CAFM on November 11, 2014.

entry.title: You Need a Mouth to Mumble
entry.content:
PaperFaces portrait of Abby B. inspired by a photograph on Sktchy.

It’s been a while since I last drew a faceless portrait, so I decided to go back to my roots with this one.

The original drawing is available on Mix if you want a closer look or to riff off of.

Stylus: Pencil by FiftyThree
App: Paper by FiftyThree

You Need a Mouth to Mumble was originally published by at CAFM on November 07, 2014.

entry.title: Let's Get Spooky
entry.content:
PaperFaces portrait of Renate W. inspired by a photograph on Sktchy.

Played with Surface Pressure and the recently updated Sketch tool in Paper tonight to create this Halloween inspired portrait. By working in reverse and applying white on black (instead of the other around) I was able to achieve a macabre feel to the piece.

The original drawing is available on Mix if you want a closer look or to riff off of.

Stylus: Pencil by FiftyThree
App: Paper by FiftyThree

Let's Get Spooky was originally published by at CAFM on October 30, 2014.

App: Paper by FiftyThree

Winter Ready was originally published by at CAFM on October 27, 2014.

entry.title: A Beard That Hides
entry.content:
PaperFaces portrait of Remi L. inspired by a photograph on Sktchy.

I started this drawing on a 3rd generation iPad with retina screen and finished it on the new iPad Air 2. The Air 2 feels so much more comfortable during long drawing sessions compared to the heavy brick that was the iPad 3.

The original drawing is available on Mix if you want a closer look or to riff off of.

Stylus: Pencil by FiftyThree
App: Paper by FiftyThree

A Beard That Hides was originally published by at CAFM on October 24, 2014.

entry.title: Rosebuds in the Pumpkin Patch
entry.content:
Last weekend Wendy and me took the girls to the Great Pumpkin Farm for the second time this autumn.

The first visit I took a bunch of photographs with my Canon EOS XTi camera but didn’t feel like lugging it around this time. So using a few snaps from my iPhone 5S I combined my favorites into a new composition and drew it in Paper for iPad.

I’m really enjoying sharing my creations on Mix and have been trying to include them at various stages of completenesss.

The best part is seeing what others do with them when remixed. I know some other creators in the Paper community have had their reservations about joining Mix. Personally the thought of someone making money off of my work or an immature kid defacing my drawings doesn’t concern me. That kind of comes with the territory in this connected world we now live in — whether you’re sharing on Mix or not.

Stylus: Pencil by FiftyThree
App: Paper by FiftyThree

Rosebuds in the Pumpkin Patch was originally published by at CAFM on October 16, 2014.

entry.title: A Face That Hides
entry.content:
PaperFaces portrait of Lisset M. inspired by a photograph on Sktchy.

For this drawing I wanted to try something a little looser than my latest drawings. Of course I couldn’t leave well enough alone and refined contours and shapes after “it was finished.”

The original drawing is available on Mix if you want a closer look or to riff on.

Stylus: Pencil by FiftyThree
App: Paper by FiftyThree

A Face That Hides was originally published by at CAFM on September 22, 2014.

entry.title: Revisiting the First PaperFace
entry.content:
Well it’s been exactly two years since I started the PaperFaces Project. Thought it would be interesting to draw my wife again to see how much my skills had improved over the course of the project.

It wasn’t the most difficult photograph to recreate in Paper, seeing how the face is obscured and all that. But you can certainly see a change in technique and accuracy between the two.

Stylus: Pencil by FiftyThree
App: Paper by FiftyThree

Revisiting the First PaperFace was originally published by at CAFM on August 29, 2014.

entry.title: Faking Pencil with Pencil
entry.content:
Each time I use Paper’s pencil tool I’m impressed with how well it performs. It doesn’t just make textured marks like every other drawing app, but is able to replicate the nuance of real colored pencils.

PaperFaces portrait of Princess E. inspired by a photograph on Sktchy.

Stylus: Pencil by FiftyThree
App: Paper by FiftyThree

Faking Pencil with Pencil was originally published by at CAFM on August 26, 2014.
